meaning: v.擦,摩擦 rt. (～ along) 和谐相处,(～ off) 擦掉,擦破,(～ out) 把(记号,笔记,笔迹等)擦掉
examples: She gave her knee a quick rub.
She sometimes asks if I want a back rub.
You may need to give the floor a rub-down with glasspaper.
To rub salt into Newcastle wounds, Liverpool used the final 20 minutes to parade Carroll.
Ideally, some of the goodwill the audience has toward the host will rub off on you.
